title: Component carrier with integrated antenna arrangement, electronic apparatus, radio communication method
abstract: It is described a component carrier (100) with a base structure (120); an antenna arrangement (140) having a at least a first antenna element (142) and a second antenna element (146), wherein both antennas elements (142, 146) are embedded within the base structure (120); and an electronic component (130), which is embedded within the base structure (120) and which is operatively connected both with the first antenna element (142) and the second antenna element (146). The electronic component (130) is an active electronic component which is capable of (i) providing a first transmit signal to the first antenna element (142) and a second transmit signal to the second antenna element (146) and/or (ii) processing a first receive signal received from the first antenna element (142) and a second receive signal received from the second antenna element (146). Further, an electronic apparatus (1070) comprising such a component carrier(100)and a manufacturing method for such a component carrier (100) are described.
claims:
Claims C L A I M S 1. A component carrier (100) comprising a base structure (120); an antenna arrangement (140) having a at least a first antenna element (142) and a second antenna element (146), wherein both antennas elements (142, 146) are embedded within the base structure (120); and an electronic component (130), which is embedded within the base structure (120) and which is operatively connected both with the first antenna element (142) and the second antenna element (146), wherein the electronic component (130) is an active electronic component which is capable of (i) providing a first transmit signal to the first antenna element (142) and a second transmit signal to the second antenna element (146) and/or (ii) processing a first receive signal received from the first antenna element (142) and a second receive signal received from the second antenna element (146). 2. The component carrier (100) as set forth in the preceding claim wherein the electronic component (130) is configured for (a) introducing a phase shift between the first transmit signal and the second transmit signal; and/or (b) changing the amplitude of the of first transmit signal and/or of the second first transmit signal; and/or (c) detecting a phase to shift between the first receive signal and the second receive signal; and/or (d) determining the amplitude of the of first receive signal and/or of the second receive transmit signal. 3. The component carrier (100) as set forth in any one of the preceding claims, further comprising 

 a first conductor trace ( 152) connecting the electronic component ( 130) with the first antenna element ( 142) and a second conductor trace ( 156) connecting the electronic component ( 130) with the second antenna element ( 146) . 4. The component carrier as set forth in the preceding claim, wherein the first conductor trace ( 152) and/or the second conductor trace ( 156) has a length of less than 600 mm, preferably less than 400 mm, more preferably less than 200 mm, more preferably less than 100 mm, more preferably less than 50 mm, more preferably less than 20 mm, more preferably less than 10 mm, and most preferably less than 5 mm . 5. The component carrier ( 100) as set forth in any one of the two preceding claims, wherein the first conductor trace ( 152) and/or the second conductor trace ( 156) consists of a number of N straight conductor trace portions ( 152a, 152b; 156a, 156b), wherein the number N is smaller than 5, in particular smaller than 4, further in particular smaller than 3, and furthermore in particular smaller than 2. 6. The component carrier ( 100) as set forth in the preceding claim, wherein the antenna arrangement ( 140) and the electronic component ( 130) are configured for operating at a predetermined wavelength and the at least one straight conductor trace portion ( 152a, 152b; 156a, 156b) has a length which is less than 1/2, preferably less than 1/4, more preferably less than 1/8, more preferably less than 1/16 mm, more preferably less than 1/32, and most preferably less than 1/64 of the predetermined wavelength . 7. The component carrier ( 100) as set forth in any one of the four preceding claims, wherein 

 the first conductor trace ( 152) and/or the second conductor trace ( 156) comprises a waveguide. 8. The component carrier (300) as set forth in any one of the preceding claims, further comprising at least one further electronic component (330b), in particular at least one further active electronic component, wherein the further electronic (330b) component is operatively connected with the electronic component (330a) . 9. The component carrier (300) as set forth in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the antenna arrangement (340) further comprises at least one further antenna element (343, 347), which is embedded within the base structure ( 120) and which is operatively connected with at least one active component (330a, 330b) . 10. The component carrier (500) as set forth in any one of the preceding claims, wherein one antenna element (542 or 546) of the antenna elements (542, 546) is formed within an opening which itself is formed at an upper surface of the base structure (420) and within the base structure (420) . 11. The component carrier (500) as set forth in the preceding claim, wherein the opening is at least partially a slot (542, 546) having a first sidewall portion and a second sidewall portion being opposite and parallel to the first sidewall portion . 12. The component carrier ( 100; 500) as set forth in one of the two preceding claims, wherein 

 an upper edge ( 142a, 146a) of the opening describes a closed line at the upper surface of the base structure ( 120; 420) . 13. The component carrier (500; 600) as set forth in any one of the three preceding claims, wherein the one antenna element (542, 546; 642, 646) is formed at least partially at a side wall of the opening . 14. The component carrier (600) as set forth in any one of the preceding claims lOto 13, wherein the opening is a passage opening (642, 646) which extends from the upper surface to an opposing lower surface of the base structure (620) . 15. The component carrier (500) as set forth in any one of the preceding claims 10 to 13, wherein the opening is a blind opening (542, 546), which extends from the upper surface of the base structure (420) into the interior of the base structure (420) . 16. The component carrier ( 100; 500) as set forth in the preceding claim, wherein the one antenna element ( 142, 146; 542, 546) is formed at least partially at a bottom wall ( 142b, 146b) of the blind opening . 17. The component carrier (200) as set forth in any one of the preceding claims 10 to 16, wherein the opening ( 142, 146) is filled at least partially with a non-conductive material (262) . 18. The component carrier as set forth in the preceding claim, wherein 

 the non-conductive material (262) is at least one of a solder resist material, a heat transfer paste, a flexi print material, a removable filling material, a paint and a lacquer. 19. The component carrier (100; 700) as set forth in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the base structure (120; 420) comprises at least a first metallic layer (124) and a second metallic layer (124) being separated by at least one dielectric layer (122) and each one of the antenna elements (142, 146; 742, 746) includes at least a part of the first metallic layer (124) and at least a part of the second metallic layer (124). 20. The component carrier (100; 700) as set forth in the preceding claim, wherein within the first metallic layer (124) one of the antenna elements (142; 742) has a first spatial spread and within the second metallic layer (124) the one of the antenna elements (142; 742) has a second spatial spread, which is different from the first spatial spread. 21. The component carrier (100) as set forth in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the base structure (120) is a layered structure comprising at least two dielectric layers (122) each being sandwiched between respectively two metal layers (124). 22. The component carrier (100) as set forth in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the base structure (120) is at least partly made from flexible materials. 23. An electronic apparatus (870, 970), in particular a radio communication device, the electronic apparatus comprising a component carrier (100) as set forth in any one of the preceding claims. 24. The electronic apparatus (970) as set forth in the preceding claim, further comprising a housing structure (972); wherein the component carrier (100) forms at least a part of the housing structure (972). 25. A method for manufacturing a component carrier (100), in particular a component carrier (100) as set forth in any one of the preceding claims 1 to 22, the method comprising providing a base structure (120); forming an antenna arrangement (140) having a at least a first antenna element (142) and a second antenna element (146), wherein both antennas elements (142, 146) are embedded within the base structure (120); embedding an electronic component (130) within the base structure (120); and connecting the electronic component (130) both with the first antenna element (142) and with the second antenna element (146), wherein the electronic component (130) is an active electronic component which is capable of (i) providing a first transmit signal to the first antenna element (142) and a second transmit signal to the second antenna element (146) and/or (ii) processing a first receive signal received from the first antenna element (142) and a second receive signal received from the second antenna element (146).
